primary_diagnosis.name: Acute symptomatic xerostomia flare in primary Sjogren's syndrome (friction dysphagia); food-bolus impaction excluded clinically

pathophysiology_summary: Primary Sjogren's syndrome is an autoimmune exocrinopathy causing dry eyes and mouth (sicca); profound xerostomia leaves too little saliva to lubricate a food bolus, so dry/crumbly food (crackers) adheres to the mucosa causing intense burning, gagging and a sensation of obstruction. This mimics an oesophageal food-bolus impaction, but the ability to swallow water and saliva with a normal voice and no drooling excludes a true bolus obstruction/airway compromise — all assessable over video. Acute suppurative sialadenitis (fever, painful parotid/submandibular swelling) and candidiasis are the other exclusions. Management is aggressive lubrication (artificial saliva), avoiding dry foods, and a bolus/parotitis safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Dry-food-triggered friction dysphagia with severe xerostomia on known Sjogren's.
Screen for inability to swallow saliva/drooling/retrosternal obstruction; absent.
Screen for fever and painful parotid/submandibular swelling; absent.
No white scrapeable plaques; erythematous dry mucosa is a risk factor to monitor.

Swallows water/saliva with normal voice and no drooling (DI-001), afebrile with no parotid swelling (DI-002), dry-food trigger on known Sjogren's relieved by lubrication (DI-003) — friction dysphagia from xerostomia.
Able to swallow water/saliva without drooling — a true bolus obstruction is excluded (safety-net retained).

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry/crumbly foods, sugar-free gum to stimulate saliva, routine rheumatology review (secretagogues), and a bolus/parotitis safety-net (can't swallow saliva/drooling; jaw swelling/fever). Ambulance/ED over-triage for a patient swallowing water with a normal voice is unnecessary.

red_flags.description: True food-bolus impaction/airway compromise — inability to swallow saliva, drooling, or voice change/stridor
Acute suppurative sialadenitis — fever with painful parotid/submandibular swelling

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Escalating a friction dysphagia to ED for presumed food-bolus obstruction despite the patient swallowing water with a normal voice.
Reassuring without screening for inability to swallow saliva/drooling (bolus) or painful glandular swelling/fever (sialadenitis).

cannot_determine_via_chat: Formal oropharyngeal/ENT examination and gland palpation
Endoscopy if a true bolus is suspected
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Artificial saliva + avoid dry foods + sugar-free gum, with a bolus/parotitis safety-net; routine rheumatology review.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging friction dysphagia wastes resources; missing a true bolus/sialadenitis would be dangerous (hence the retained safety-net).
